reviews_data.review_text:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
Purrfect Pair Cat Cafe is absolutely incredible! 💯
From cozy movie nights surrounded by the sweetest cats to having my precious baby 🐈‍⬛ lovingly cared for during 4 days of their awesome boarding, every single experience has been nothing short of amazing!

The staff truly goes above and beyond to make you feel welcome, comfortable, and confident that your fur baby is in the best hands! You can feel the love and care they pour into everything they do. Warm, welcoming, and unforgettable, this place is a gem 👏🏼💙

Highly recommend to any cat lover!

reviews_data.owner_answer:
Thank you so much for your kind review!  We love your boys and we were so glad to see them again!  They were so affectionate and playful, our staff loved to be back there with them!  
I’m so glad you trusted us for your cat boarding needs. Can’t wait to see them again, and see you in March for our new menu!
reviews_data.review_text:
We love Purrfect Pair Cat Cafe! Most recently, we boarded our two cats for 8 nights while we went on vacation. Inga and her staff sent plenty of updates and photos of them while we were traveling, and we were completely confident in their care. The facility is set up well with our two bonded kitties able to interact during their stay. The booking and payment processes were very easy. They were handled online, and the reservation was confirmed promptly. We will use the boarding services again.

We do love the cat cafe for more than just boarding. Our cats are alumni that we adopted from Purrfect Pair and were fostered through their rescue partner. We love to visit to see the residents, try out drinks and snacks (love the rotating monthly specialty drink menu!), buy new cat toys, and play board games. Purrfect Pair is a Rock Hill treasure!

reviews_data.review_text: Really enjoyed this experience! The strawberry latte drink that I had was amazingly delicious and I truly enjoyed meeting and playing with the cats and kittens in the lounge. The staff is friendly and helpful. Will definitely be returning soon!

reviews_data.review_text:
Wow! I can’t say enough great things about this place! The employees and owner are all so nice and helpful. We are looking to adopt cats and I came in to ask questions. Not only were all my questions answered, they helped me through the application process.

I have done the cat experience before, and it was fun to meet all the fur babies.

I have not taken my daughter yet, but I saw moms with preschoolers having a great time.

Even if you just want a coffee or cat themed gift, it’s a great place to go. You don’t have to do the cat experience to shop.

I highly recommend checking this place out!

reviews_data.review_text: Best day of my life!!!! I hadn't ever been to a cat café before which I told the purrista and she personally introduced me to all the kitties. Everything is clean and well-maintained and it's clear that the cats are very loved. I went in the middle of the day so most of the older cats were snoozing but I did spend a delightful hour with a little kitten menace who kept fighting the strings on my hoodie. If you want to play with the older cats, plan to show up later in the afternoon or more towards the evening hours. The drink I got was great and there's tons of adorable merch (they support local artists!!!). Even better, this place is LGBT+ friendly! I highly recommend this place to all cat lovers. I will definitely be back!

Had the best time visiting the cat lounge! We made a 30 min reservation online, super easy and quick to book in advance or on short notice.

The rules for the kids were straightforward in that kids couldnt pick up the cats but could sit and have a cat placed on their lap. There were enough cats that my kids enjoyed walking around petting all the different kitties. All the cats were super sweet and friendly. When you don’t or can’t have your own cat, Purrfect Pair is the ideal place to get your fill of spending time with kitties.

Also the employees were incredibly courteous and helpful. We got a cappuccino, macchiato, strawberry lemonade, and cookies on our way out. The drinks, especially the espresso and cappuccino, were not only reasonably priced but very good. Exceeded my expectations, can’t wait to return!

reviews_data.owner_answer:
We are very sorry to hear that this was your experience. We do everything to ensure that each cat goes home with the right family, and part of that process is trying to honor first-come, first-served; when it makes sense. We do not allow our staff to discuss the applications of others with customers because we have found that it avoids unnecessary conflict for our staff. 

What delayed your boyfriend’s application was that the veterinarian refused to give us info on if your current pet was up to date on vaccinations over the phone, and insisted on emailing records, which were never received.  When you and your boyfriend came to the cafe last he asked about the application and I told him we were still waiting; however he was able to email me the records immediately. 

As for the demographic we adopt to, that is a far-fetched allegation as we continue to and have adopted out to a wide range of individuals. We pride ourselves on being an inclusive environment at Purrfect Pair and always doing what is in the best interest of the cats.

Dawson went home with a wonderful couple who are passionate about their cats and happened to have an application in with records received before the one your boyfriend submitted.
reviews_data.review_text:
My experience with this cat café was disappointing, to say the least.
Initially, my visit was pleasant I brought my boyfriend, and we both fell in love with one of the cats. However, several issues quickly became apparent. My boyfriend completed the adoption process but was never informed that there were other applicants for the same cat. After waiting nearly two weeks, we were abruptly told that the cat had been placed with another family. There was a complete lack of communication throughout the process; we had to repeatedly follow up and even remind the owner about required paperwork.
What’s more troubling is a noticeable pattern on the café’s social media. After reviewing their Instagram page, I couldn’t help but observe that the vast majority of families shown adopting were of a certain demographic  with very few families of color represented. Whether intentional or not, that speaks volumes and raises serious concerns about fairness and inclusivity in their adoption practices.
Overall, this experience left my boyfriend, his family, and myself deeply disappointed. I strongly advise potential adopters to proceed with caution, document all communication, and hold the business accountable for transparency and professionalism

Update: Wow, im shocked, but not surprised about these one star reviews and how the place is run.  When I went there few months ago, thankfully I had free coupon.  Only 2 cats!!  Others were hiding.   Also told by employees the owner constantly changes organizations, none in York County to get cats.  How they stay open i dont get it.   Progress to opening, she was gonna open at a different location.   There was too much red tape.  It was on East Mt.Gallant accross from Manchester Meadows.   I got her name from the owner at a cat café in Georgetown.   I was going to donate until I saw she donated $$$ to , cant even put it, so ashamed.  This was around two and a half years ago.

The vibe wasn't there.  Felt cramped.  I love the Georgetown location better.  There it's only $12 an hour with a free beverage!!  And I loved the volunteers there.  RH, take note!!

Update...no longer communication with Humane Society.   Found out the real reason why.  Won't associate with the shelter either.  Goes through an agency in a county in NC.  Not cool.  Oops, now through a shelter in Seneca, SC.  Too bad not on own county!!  Disgusting!!

Disgusting being open on Easter and advertising on the Lost and Found Pet site.  Spam.  It's a site for lost and found pets, not business.

UPDATE: To the influx of new reviewers suggesting that the cats are better off at Purrfect Pair than at a crowded shelter or "outside neglected by a dumpster," I AGREE 100%. However, the point is to allow these cats to be adopted if the right person comes along and shows interest in doing so. Please don't force them to stay in the cafe for the rest of their lives if they have an opportunity to be loved and spoiled by a caring family/individual. I made this post because I care for the long term well-being of these cats, not to be malicious in any way. It's obvious that I don't stand alone in my concern.

— ORIGINAL REVIEW BELOW —

I am extremely disappointed in this cat cafe. Although I did not originally agree with some of their practices (i.e., allowing customers to pick the cats up), it appeared as though their intentions were good enough when they opened last year. It really seemed like they had their hearts in the right place in terms of finding forever homes for their cats. HOWEVER. I was recently made aware that they have since adopted a total of SEVEN cats into their cafe -- they call them their 'purrmanent residents.' This is absolutely despicable and not at all in the best interest of the animals.

One reviewer mentioned that Latte (one of the seven) loves people but is very skittish around the other cats. The owner justified keeping her by saying that she is bonded with her sister (another 'purrmanent resident'), as if nobody would possibly be willing to adopt a bonded pair. Her solution to the anxious behaviors exhibited by Latte were to put her in a 'calming collar,' as she has seemingly done with most (if not all) of the 'purrmanent residents.'

The owner also admitted that the reason for keeping these 'purrmanent residents' is that, "we are often adopting out cats more quickly than we can get them in..." -- this was in response to a concerned reviewer, stating that staff had mentioned they could not stay open without keeping a 'base set' of cats. Whose best interest are they truly looking out for -- the cats or the business? She also stated that on top of the 'base set' of seven 'purrmanent residents,' they are allowed to host up to 13 more (adoptable) cats at a time. That means, at any given moment, there may be a total of TWENTY cats confined together in a very small area.

While I do not fully agree with keeping even one cat as a 'purrmanent resident,' I acknowledge that it can be helpful at times for new (and nervous) arrivals to interact with a calm, laid-back resident animal. However, to quote a concerned reviewer, "[Latte] constantly gets bullied by other cats, even by Mr. Foster (who belongs to the owner of the cafe I was told)." This causes me to wonder why Mr. Foster was chosen as the original 'purrmanent resident' of Purrfect Pair, as bullying isn't exactly a preferred trait of any well-suited resident animal. (Nothing against Mr. Foster -- I'm sure he is very sweet in other circumstances!)

Overall, I do not trust the judgement of this establishment owner. While Purrfect Pair has done a lot of good in terms of adoptions (145 since opening last year), they seem to be going backward by forcing these cats to stay with them indefinitely. I can only hope that the owner will see reason and allow these cats to experience the pure love, joy, and fulfillment that waits for them in their forever homes.

————

**Note: Since leaving my review on Friday, the number of ‘purrmanent residents’ has gone up. Eight cats were featured in a recent social media post, and Mr. Foster (assuming he’s still there) makes a total of nine.

reviews_data.owner_answer: Thank you for your review.  We appreciate your concerns, and the health and well being of our purrmanent residents are top priority.  They have regular vet visits, plenty of snuggles, and a huge room with lots of toys and enrichment.  They also have a separate room where they have their litter boxes, extra food, and beds and cat trees.  We still have staff that come in for deep cleaning on the days we are closed, so they never go even 12 hours without human contact. If the purrmanent residents ever show signs that they are not happy or healthy we will reevaluate their status.  The staff was correct that we are often adopting out cats more quickly than we can get them in; our purrmament residents are our 'base' set of cats and then we can have up to 13 more cats that are up for adoption.  We've adopted 145 cats out to forever homes in the time that we've been open with three different rescues/shelters.  All of our purrmanent cats have come from shelters and rescues; and often serve as good role models for the adoptable cats that come through the cafe. we got a 30-min reservation with a young child and a toddler. all went fine. the owner/assistants were nice. the environment is clean and nice and have enough space. you can't expect more than this.

I saw some bad comments but ppl should know how to enter "cats" world. at least three below:
1- they are not your servants to give you joy! cats are quite different than dogs. you can't force them to play or command them sit/wait. they choose whether to play.
2- their sleeping schedule is quite different then ours (i.e. when we got most of them were sleepy ~ 3pm, a quite normal time for them sleep). don't force them to wake up. how would you respond if someone forces to wake up at 3 am?
3- imagine you meet 5-10 people every 30 minutes. can you be nice always to all of them? they also need some space to relax. your kid/nephew/grandkid (etc) can get cranky during the day.

UPDATE:
I'm updating my post because there was a one star post left by someone who was angry about one of the resident kittens being bullied and scared.
As someone who frequents the cafe a few times a week I can assure you there was no bullying of any cats. The two kittens were new to the lounge and were acclimating to the new environment.

In regards to Latte, her and her sister, Mocha, came from a confiscation situation via CMPD and it was best for them to be kept together, which we were able to accommodate. They are still very new to the lounge and Latte is a bit more reserved than her sister, however our staff, including a previous vet tech, monitor the cats health and well-being very closely and would never put them in a situation that is not in their best interest. Verde, our long term resident, also took time to warm up to the space, but it became her home and she treats it as such. While our lounge may not be a good fit for all cats, in which case we would not force it, Latte is very sweet and outgoing. In all scenarios with cats it takes time to get used to a new environment especially when coming from a bad situation like she did. We apologize if you were interested in adopting Latte, but there are plenty of other kitties that would love a forever home. reviews_data.review_text:
Update: 04/26/2025
I just read your response to my review,  I want to clear out that I didn't want to adopt "latte". I mentioned I rescue and foster cats and dogs, therfore I'm not looking to adopt any animal. My mission is to find cats and dogs good homes.
I understand what you said but you also have to keep in mind that the cafe should not be something permanent for Latte and her sister, I'm sure that at some point you will be able to find someone that will adopt the sisters together. Like I said that environment no matter how good you may think it is for her,  it's shouldn't be permanent if you want what's best for her. She constantly gets bullied by the other cats, even by Mr Foster (who belongs to the owner of the cafe i was told).
I was stating my honest opinion.
Have a nice day.

***
***I was super excited to go to "purrfect pair cat cafe", I walked in this morning with my friends. We got our coffees and paid 10 per person (for 30 minutes) all the cats looked well taken care of, there was food and water, the bowls were spotless, the cats had lots of toys and places to lay and sleep. BUT the reason why I'm leaving a 1 star review is because of "latte" the sweet 6 month old, black female cat. She and another one (i don't remember the second cat's name) were not up for adoption,  they're actually permanent residents (that's what I read on their cards and I was also told the same thing by the staff) my question is WHY? Latte was extremely sweet and social with PEOPLE but she was terrified of the other cats, she was constantly hiding and running away from the rest of the cats. They were all bullying her and the poor thing had nowhere to hide. When I walked in the area, I didn't see her. After 20 minutes of my friends and staying in there and petting all the cats, we spotted a frightened Latte hiding behind the couch.  When my friend approached her, Latte started meowing and immediately let us pet her and hold her. A cat that's so friendly and sweet with people should NOT stay in there for their rest of her life, she deserves to find a good home and live in a stress free environment where she doesn't have to watch her back 24/7. I rescue and foster cats and dogs (mainly cats) and Latte broke my heart. She and the other "permanent resident " don't deserve this.  Keep in mind that when a cat is stressed out for long periods of time they can get sick... This is terrible, I'm never going to that cat cafe and I will never support them again.

I have never gotten sick because a barista forgot to substitute my milk for almond milk like I have from Starbucks. The baristas are good at their jobs. They are friendly and always make good drinks. I'm so happy that Purrfect Pair opened.

reviews_data.review_text: The staff is friendly and well... they have a bunch of cats, what more reason could you need?? They also have a lot of cute items for sale that seem to be sourced locally. I purchased a couple pairs of earrings, a cute little phone stand, and a sticker notebook. Everything was reasonably priced and good quality. There's also a great drink menu but I didn't try it out this visit. We browsed around a bit while waiting for our appointment time. I like that you can see the cats while in the main area and learn about them while you're waiting. They had about 15 cats if I counted correctly. Most were sleeping during our visit but there were a few that were willing to play. You can tell they're all well socialized and used to getting attention all day long as many were not interested in interacting. We also visited in the middle of the day and having owned cats before, I know how much they love their beauty rest and tend to be most active at night. So just be aware that you may find yourself wandering around trying to find a cat that is awake and willing to play or receive pets. They seem well cared for and the room has tons of toys and games for both humans and cats to enjoy. Everything was clean and I appreciate that they had a designated area for litter that was not within the visiting room. There was no unpleasant odors, which I know is always a concern with animals. There seems to have been a lot of thought that went into the set up and tons of cat themed items every where you look. I imagine it's a great hangout spot during the week when it's not as busy. I love supporting local businesses and especially those who help animals find their perfect home so I would recommend checking it out if you ever have the chance.

reviews_data.owner_answer: Sending you such a big hug right now for this- it made my day!  Thank you for being an awesome customer!
reviews_data.review_text:
I've become quite a frequent visitor here so figured it was time to leave a review!

For the Cafe Side - the drinks are delicious and you can always customize if you want something specific. There are generic snacks as well as stuff that is from local bakeries (which I prefer. The pumpkin whoopie pie is dangerously good!) The selection isn't huge for snacks but there's enough that most people will be able to find something they'd like. I also get that due to health code reasons, food has to come prepackaged.
There's also a good variety of merchandise you can buy. The shirts fit well and are exceptionally soft. I hope more designs are offered in the future. There are a few AI items which, as an artist, isn't my favorite, but they've been moving toward showcasing local artists which is awesome! It is a small area but that's due to the building itself over theirs.

For the Cat Lounge - I love how all the cats are adoptable! It's truly a great way to find them homes. If anything, I think they should implement cupholders in the tables or such as the cats do want to knock them over but that's more a want than a need. If you plan on going, i highly suggest making an appointment ahead of time as walk ins are never guaranteed to have space. The couches are comfy but they also have tables that are nice to sit at too! The chairs at the tables don't have cushions but they don't hurt to sit on. Cushions could be a nice addition, though I'm sure the cats would make more use of them than people could, haha. I like the amount of rules they have that keep the cats safe and comfy

The Staff - all of the employees I've met have been great. I want to highlight Sophia, Sydney, Laurie, Annalise and of course the owner Inga have all been so kind and are part of the reason I've been so comfortable visiting so often

So overall, go visit and get a coffee!!

Mismanaged. Called and made an appointment at 3:30pm. Traffic was heavy and we were 5 mins late. They told us to wait until 4pm. No one was in there.

We waited 25 minutes, sipping coffee quietly, and then it was a big crowd at 4pm, with 4 little children. Never called our group - we looked up to see people in the room. Then staff insisted on doing small groups to review the rules. They literally let all these walk ins ahead of us! The cats were overwhelmed at the chaos and noise and hid. Some were hissing and one scratched my son. I don’t know why they just didn’t honor our original reservation and managed the crowd better - for the sake of the patrons and the cats.

ETA: I did speak to them before I left - they were blasé. We spend about $40 for 2 coffees and 30 mins with the cats. Definitely the quality of the mismanaged experience was not worth the price.

reviews_data.review_text:
Pro's: Really nice owner/manager.
Incredible mission to care for the animals and do what they can to get the kitties adopted.
Really cool merch. A variety of options for merchandise.
Awesome decor and options for seating for 1-5 groups of people.
Amazing cat view window.
Cool bar space to work in front of the large window and watch the cats while working on a laptop or whatever else...
Incredible living space for the kitties.
Options for kids drinks.
Delicious drinks from the bar. I was content with my chai tea latte. The young girls enjoyed the sweets and the slushy drinks.
Vegan/milk substitute latte options.
Clear rules in place.
Cons: Really small hosting space in the cafe. It was very packed on Saturday afternoon, which is good for business but difficult for people who want to sit down and hang out for a while.
It is very challenging to seat more than 4-5 people together. The 4 very comfortable sofa chairs are cool but take up too much space. Would love to see a couple small round tables with a few chairs per each one.
The slushy machine makes it very difficult to talk because it was so loud.
Only options for really sweet treats and no options for healthy snacks, sugar free or low sugar snacks.
No food other than treats to go with your drink and if you're there to see the kitties, you can't take your drink with you for the half hour you're paying to hang out with them.
You have to pay per individual not a group to go in to visit the cats.
The fee is very high for a long period of time. There's no affordable option for a 5 minute visit.
My chai latte was good, but average, not great. It was mostly tea and milk. I could barely taste any kind of spices. So, if I go in I'll have to try a different drink next time.
Thank you for hosting us, We enjoyed our time there, but will definitely have to visit with a small group next time.

We are very sorry to hear that this was your experience. We do everything to ensure that each cat goes home with the right family, and part of that process is trying to honor first-come, first-served; when it makes sense. We do not allow our staff to discuss the applications of others with customers because we have found that it avoids unnecessary conflict for our staff. 

What delayed your boyfriend’s application was that the veterinarian refused to give us info on if your current pet was up to date on vaccinations over the phone, and insisted on emailing records, which were never received.  When you and your boyfriend came to the cafe last he asked about the application and I told him we were still waiting; however he was able to email me the records immediately. 

As for the demographic we adopt to, that is a far-fetched allegation as we continue to and have adopted out to a wide range of individuals. We pride ourselves on being an inclusive environment at Purrfect Pair and always doing what is in the best interest of the cats.

Dawson went home with a wonderful couple who are passionate about their cats and happened to have an application in with records received before the one your boyfriend submitted.
reviews_data.review_text:
My experience with this cat café was disappointing, to say the least.
Initially, my visit was pleasant I brought my boyfriend, and we both fell in love with one of the cats. However, several issues quickly became apparent. My boyfriend completed the adoption process but was never informed that there were other applicants for the same cat. After waiting nearly two weeks, we were abruptly told that the cat had been placed with another family. There was a complete lack of communication throughout the process; we had to repeatedly follow up and even remind the owner about required paperwork.
What’s more troubling is a noticeable pattern on the café’s social media. After reviewing their Instagram page, I couldn’t help but observe that the vast majority of families shown adopting were of a certain demographic  with very few families of color represented. Whether intentional or not, that speaks volumes and raises serious concerns about fairness and inclusivity in their adoption practices.
Overall, this experience left my boyfriend, his family, and myself deeply disappointed. I strongly advise potential adopters to proceed with caution, document all communication, and hold the business accountable for transparency and professionalism

reviews_data.review_text: An amazing small, locally-owned business in Rock Hill! 🐱💜

UPDATE: To the influx of new reviewers suggesting that the cats are better off at Purrfect Pair than at a crowded shelter or "outside neglected by a dumpster," I AGREE 100%. However, the point is to allow these cats to be adopted if the right person comes along and shows interest in doing so. Please don't force them to stay in the cafe for the rest of their lives if they have an opportunity to be loved and spoiled by a caring family/individual. I made this post because I care for the long term well-being of these cats, not to be malicious in any way. It's obvious that I don't stand alone in my concern.

— ORIGINAL REVIEW BELOW —

I am extremely disappointed in this cat cafe. Although I did not originally agree with some of their practices (i.e., allowing customers to pick the cats up), it appeared as though their intentions were good enough when they opened last year. It really seemed like they had their hearts in the right place in terms of finding forever homes for their cats. HOWEVER. I was recently made aware that they have since adopted a total of SEVEN cats into their cafe -- they call them their 'purrmanent residents.' This is absolutely despicable and not at all in the best interest of the animals.

One reviewer mentioned that Latte (one of the seven) loves people but is very skittish around the other cats. The owner justified keeping her by saying that she is bonded with her sister (another 'purrmanent resident'), as if nobody would possibly be willing to adopt a bonded pair. Her solution to the anxious behaviors exhibited by Latte were to put her in a 'calming collar,' as she has seemingly done with most (if not all) of the 'purrmanent residents.'

The owner also admitted that the reason for keeping these 'purrmanent residents' is that, "we are often adopting out cats more quickly than we can get them in..." -- this was in response to a concerned reviewer, stating that staff had mentioned they could not stay open without keeping a 'base set' of cats. Whose best interest are they truly looking out for -- the cats or the business? She also stated that on top of the 'base set' of seven 'purrmanent residents,' they are allowed to host up to 13 more (adoptable) cats at a time. That means, at any given moment, there may be a total of TWENTY cats confined together in a very small area.

While I do not fully agree with keeping even one cat as a 'purrmanent resident,' I acknowledge that it can be helpful at times for new (and nervous) arrivals to interact with a calm, laid-back resident animal. However, to quote a concerned reviewer, "[Latte] constantly gets bullied by other cats, even by Mr. Foster (who belongs to the owner of the cafe I was told)." This causes me to wonder why Mr. Foster was chosen as the original 'purrmanent resident' of Purrfect Pair, as bullying isn't exactly a preferred trait of any well-suited resident animal. (Nothing against Mr. Foster -- I'm sure he is very sweet in other circumstances!)

Overall, I do not trust the judgement of this establishment owner. While Purrfect Pair has done a lot of good in terms of adoptions (145 since opening last year), they seem to be going backward by forcing these cats to stay with them indefinitely. I can only hope that the owner will see reason and allow these cats to experience the pure love, joy, and fulfillment that waits for them in their forever homes.

————

**Note: Since leaving my review on Friday, the number of ‘purrmanent residents’ has gone up. Eight cats were featured in a recent social media post, and Mr. Foster (assuming he’s still there) makes a total of nine.

If you think the café’s practices are harmful, you clearly haven’t spent real time there. As someone who visits regularly—not just once for a cute Instagram post—I can tell you the cats are happy. The big windows, the peaceful space, and the constant love they get? It’s more than most cats could ever ask for. And guess what? Not having a single “owner” isn’t neglect—it’s freedom. That’s how cats lived before humans started acting like they know best.

So here’s the truth: a small business in Rock Hill that’s doing something kind shouldn’t be the target of bitterness. You can’t tear it down just because you’re loud online. There are too many of us who see through the lies, and we’re not going anywhere.

reviews_data.owner_answer: Thank you for your review.  We appreciate your concerns, and the health and well being of our purrmanent residents are top priority.  They have regular vet visits, plenty of snuggles, and a huge room with lots of toys and enrichment.  They also have a separate room where they have their litter boxes, extra food, and beds and cat trees.  We still have staff that come in for deep cleaning on the days we are closed, so they never go even 12 hours without human contact. If the purrmanent residents ever show signs that they are not happy or healthy we will reevaluate their status.  The staff was correct that we are often adopting out cats more quickly than we can get them in; our purrmament residents are our 'base' set of cats and then we can have up to 13 more cats that are up for adoption.  We've adopted 145 cats out to forever homes in the time that we've been open with three different rescues/shelters.  All of our purrmanent cats have come from shelters and rescues; and often serve as good role models for the adoptable cats that come through the cafe.  We aim to have more adoptables from our rescue soon as they meet the age and medical requirements we need to keep all cats happy and healthy.

we got a 30-min reservation with a young child and a toddler. all went fine. the owner/assistants were nice. the environment is clean and nice and have enough space. you can't expect more than this.

I saw some bad comments but ppl should know how to enter "cats" world. at least three below:
1- they are not your servants to give you joy! cats are quite different than dogs. you can't force them to play or command them sit/wait. they choose whether to play.
2- their sleeping schedule is quite different then ours (i.e. when we got most of them were sleepy ~ 3pm, a quite normal time for them sleep). don't force them to wake up. how would you respond if someone forces to wake up at 3 am?
3- imagine you meet 5-10 people every 30 minutes. can you be nice always to all of them? they also need some space to relax. your kid/nephew/grandkid (etc) can get cranky during the day.

I LOVE this place!!! What a hidden treasure in Rock Hill. The staff is friendly and knowledgeable,  the custom menus that change monthly are delicious 😋.  My new favorite  is the Merry Berry, chai cranberry drink that is the purrfect  winter drink.

As a mom, I love all of the child friendly events they hold. Swiftember was a Taylor Swift theme for September.  November was Harry Potter month, and now its all about the holidays.

Their boutique offers an array of sweet cat themed treasures made by local artists and artisans. Local businesses helping each other, love it!

The best part, is the cat lounge. What an amazing place for kitties to play, lounge, and make friends. This place has ZERO cat odor and all the kitties are up for adoption!!

Don't forget to say hi to Verde, the Cafe mascot.

UPDATE:
I'm updating my post because there was a one star post left by someone who was angry about one of the resident kittens being bullied and scared.
As someone who frequents the cafe a few times a week I can assure you there was no bullying of any cats. The two kittens were new to the lounge and were acclimating to the new environment.
Also, the kitten was NOT hiding behind the sofa. She was hanging out on the cat tree behind the sofa. BIG DIFFERENCE!!!!
Shame on this woman for leaving this review and trying to deter anyone from coming here. This place provides a safe, loving home for resident kitties and for those waiting to find their forever homes.

reviews_data.owner_answer: Thank you so much for your kind words about our space. In regards to Latte, her and her sister, Mocha, came from a confiscation situation via CMPD and it was best for them to be kept together, which we were able to accommodate. They are still very new to the lounge and Latte is a bit more reserved than her sister, however our staff, including a previous vet tech, monitor the cats health and well-being very closely and would never put them in a situation that is not in their best interest. Verde, our long term resident, also took time to warm up to the space, but it became her home and she treats it as such. While our lounge may not be a good fit for all cats, in which case we would not force it, Latte is very sweet and outgoing. In all scenarios with cats it takes time to get used to a new environment especially when coming from a bad situation like she did. We apologize if you were interested in adopting Latte, but there are plenty of other kitties that would love a forever home. If you have further questions please don’t hesitate to reach out to info@purrfectpaircatcafe.com
reviews_data.review_text:
Update: 04/26/2025
I just read your response to my review,  I want to clear out that I didn't want to adopt "latte". I mentioned I rescue and foster cats and dogs, therfore I'm not looking to adopt any animal. My mission is to find cats and dogs good homes.
I understand what you said but you also have to keep in mind that the cafe should not be something permanent for Latte and her sister, I'm sure that at some point you will be able to find someone that will adopt the sisters together. Like I said that environment no matter how good you may think it is for her,  it's shouldn't be permanent if you want what's best for her. She constantly gets bullied by the other cats, even by Mr Foster (who belongs to the owner of the cafe i was told).
I was stating my honest opinion.
Have a nice day.

***
***I was super excited to go to "purrfect pair cat cafe", I walked in this morning with my friends. We got our coffees and paid 10 per person (for 30 minutes) all the cats looked well taken care of, there was food and water, the bowls were spotless, the cats had lots of toys and places to lay and sleep. BUT the reason why I'm leaving a 1 star review is because of "latte" the sweet 6 month old, black female cat. She and another one (i don't remember the second cat's name) were not up for adoption,  they're actually permanent residents (that's what I read on their cards and I was also told the same thing by the staff) my question is WHY? Latte was extremely sweet and social with PEOPLE but she was terrified of the other cats, she was constantly hiding and running away from the rest of the cats. They were all bullying her and the poor thing had nowhere to hide. When I walked in the area, I didn't see her. After 20 minutes of my friends and staying in there and petting all the cats, we spotted a frightened Latte hiding behind the couch.  When my friend approached her, Latte started meowing and immediately let us pet her and hold her. A cat that's so friendly and sweet with people should NOT stay in there for their rest of her life, she deserves to find a good home and live in a stress free environment where she doesn't have to watch her back 24/7. I rescue and foster cats and dogs (mainly cats) and Latte broke my heart. She and the other "permanent resident " don't deserve this.  Keep in mind that when a cat is stressed out for long periods of time they can get sick... This is terrible, I'm never going to that cat cafe and I will never support them again.

reviews_data.owner_answer: Thank you!  Your review was so sweet and makes our hearts happy!
reviews_data.review_text:
I am in love with this place. I stop by at least once a month to try something from the new menu and say hello to new and old friends. I love keeping track of all the kitties and their adoptions. I can't say I don't have favorites though. I ALWAYS say hello to my girl Verde. The cats are always happy and well taken care of. Everyone there truly cares about the wellbeing of the kittens. I love seeing the community and how many cats have gotten loving homes from the cafe.

I love the rotating menu. The coffee is well priced. It is lower cost than Starbucks and without fail higher quality. I have never gotten sick because a barista forgot to substitute my milk for almond milk like I have from Starbucks. The baristas are good at their jobs. They are friendly and always make good drinks. I've been there for hours just making conversation.

I have followed the cafe since it was a GoFundMe at Rock Hill Pride. The cat cafe has been the perfect addition to Rock Hill and a beautiful place for the community to center around. It's perfect for families and very kid friendly. It's also perfect for a cozy date. I love coming to the cafe with my partner and enjoying the cats and good coffee.

The owner, Inga, is a wonderful human being. She has poured her heart and soul into this cafe and it shows. Every detail is attended too.

I'm so happy that Purrfect Pair opened.

reviews_data.review_text: The staff is friendly and well... they have a bunch of cats, what more reason could you need?? They also have a lot of cute items for sale that seem to be sourced locally. I purchased a couple pairs of earrings, a cute little phone stand, and a sticker notebook. Everything was reasonably priced and good quality. There's also a great drink menu but I didn't try it out this visit. We browsed around a bit while waiting for our appointment time. I like that you can see the cats while in the main area and learn about them while you're waiting. They had about 15 cats if I counted correctly. Most were sleeping during our visit but there were a few that were willing to play. You can tell they're all well socialized and used to getting attention all day long as many were not interested in interacting. We also visited in the middle of the day and having owned cats before, I know how much they love their beauty rest and tend to be most active at night. So just be aware that you may find yourself wandering around trying to find a cat that is awake and willing to play or receive pets. They seem well cared for and the room has tons of toys and games for both humans and cats to enjoy. Everything was clean and I appreciate that they had a designated area for litter that was not within the visiting room. There was no unpleasant odors, which I know is always a concern with animals. There seems to have been a lot of thought that went into the set up and tons of cat themed items every where you look. I imagine it's a great hangout spot during the week when it's not as busy. I love supporting local businesses and especially those who help animals find their perfect home so I would recommend checking it out if you ever have the chance.

reviews_data.owner_answer: Sending you such a big hug right now for this- it made my day!  Thank you for being an awesome customer!
reviews_data.review_text:
I've become quite a frequent visitor here so figured it was time to leave a review!

For the Cafe Side - the drinks are delicious and you can always customize if you want something specific. There are generic snacks as well as stuff that is from local bakeries (which I prefer. The pumpkin whoopie pie is dangerously good!) The selection isn't huge for snacks but there's enough that most people will be able to find something they'd like. I also get that due to health code reasons, food has to come prepackaged.
There's also a good variety of merchandise you can buy. The shirts fit well and are exceptionally soft. I hope more designs are offered in the future. There are a few AI items which, as an artist, isn't my favorite, but they've been moving toward showcasing local artists which is awesome! It is a small area but that's due to the building itself over theirs.

For the Cat Lounge - I love how all the cats are adoptable! It's truly a great way to find them homes. If anything, I think they should implement cupholders in the tables or such as the cats do want to knock them over but that's more a want than a need. If you plan on going, i highly suggest making an appointment ahead of time as walk ins are never guaranteed to have space. The couches are comfy but they also have tables that are nice to sit at too! The chairs at the tables don't have cushions but they don't hurt to sit on. Cushions could be a nice addition, though I'm sure the cats would make more use of them than people could, haha. I like the amount of rules they have that keep the cats safe and comfy

The Staff - all of the employees I've met have been great. I want to highlight Sophia, Sydney, Laurie, Annalise and of course the owner Inga have all been so kind and are part of the reason I've been so comfortable visiting so often

So overall, go visit and get a coffee!!

reviews_data.review_text:
Mismanaged. Called and made an appointment at 3:30pm. Traffic was heavy and we were 5 mins late. They told us to wait until 4pm. No one was in there.

We waited 25 minutes, sipping coffee quietly, and then it was a big crowd at 4pm, with 4 little children. Never called our group - we looked up to see people in the room. Then staff insisted on doing small groups to review the rules. They literally let all these walk ins ahead of us! The cats were overwhelmed at the chaos and noise and hid. Some were hissing and one scratched my son. I don’t know why they just didn’t honor our original reservation and managed the crowd better - for the sake of the patrons and the cats.

ETA: I did speak to them before I left - they were blasé. We spend about $40 for 2 coffees and 30 mins with the cats. Definitely the quality of the mismanaged experience was not worth the price.

reviews_data.owner_answer: Hello, and thank you for visiting and writing a review for our small business. I did want to clarify some things that are incorrect from your review.  You can take food/drinks into the cat lounge but we ask you to monitor them carefully since the cats will try to knock them over.  We do have the Skinny Pop Popcorn as a healthy-ish option.  We can only offer pre-packaged items since live animals are in the building per health code.  We offer a group rate but it must be scheduled ahead of time.  I too wish we had a bigger space to allow a more private area for parties or gatherings, maybe at our next location!  :-)
reviews_data.review_text:
Pro's: Really nice owner/manager.
Incredible mission to care for the animals and do what they can to get the kitties adopted.
Really cool merch. A variety of options for merchandise.
Awesome decor and options for seating for 1-5 groups of people.
Amazing cat view window.
Cool bar space to work in front of the large window and watch the cats while working on a laptop or whatever else...
Incredible living space for the kitties.
Options for kids drinks.
Delicious drinks from the bar. I was content with my chai tea latte. The young girls enjoyed the sweets and the slushy drinks.
Vegan/milk substitute latte options.
Clear rules in place.
Cons: Really small hosting space in the cafe. It was very packed on Saturday afternoon, which is good for business but difficult for people who want to sit down and hang out for a while.
It is very challenging to seat more than 4-5 people together. The 4 very comfortable sofa chairs are cool but take up too much space. Would love to see a couple small round tables with a few chairs per each one.
The slushy machine makes it very difficult to talk because it was so loud.
Only options for really sweet treats and no options for healthy snacks, sugar free or low sugar snacks.
No food other than treats to go with your drink and if you're there to see the kitties, you can't take your drink with you for the half hour you're paying to hang out with them.
You have to pay per individual not a group to go in to visit the cats.
The fee is very high for a long period of time. There's no affordable option for a 5 minute visit.
My chai latte was good, but average, not great. It was mostly tea and milk. I could barely taste any kind of spices. So, if I go in I'll have to try a different drink next time.
Thank you for hosting us, We enjoyed our time there, but will definitely have to visit with a small group next time.

reviews_data.owner_answer: Thank you so much for the kind review!  We are working on a monthly pass so you can even more! Thank you for pointing out the issue with scheduling- we will troubleshoot it.
reviews_data.review_text:
I adore this cat cafe!! I've been over five times at this point, want to make it a weekly visit. All the cats come from the local Humane Society, and all the proceeds go towards supporting the shelter!! I hope you'll visit if only to keep supporting their work and taking care of our community's cats.
The cafe itself is beautiful, very well maintained and has a lovely purple aesthetic. Great music, great drink/food offerings, and the staff are great.
The only hiccup I had was in booking a visit for myself and three other people; we were late due to traffic by about 10 minutes, and by the time we got there, all the spaces were filled despite our reserving the time. I think in the future if there's a way to put a notice that the reservation will only hold for a certain amount of time before the spots are relinquished, that'd be GREAT!
Thankfully the staff refunded me and I've been mindful of scheduling timing for future visit. Haven't had an issue since.
